speaker: Lora Albright
words: And that's where he's buried over there. And, mother, read the verses from the Bible, from memory. And Raleigh, put the baby in the casket in. His mother told me afterwards. words: But we did put a little bronze thing on the tree because he was buried in that big tree. So he's around the homestead. But it was a difficult thing. words: It was a touchy thing for him for a long time, because he felt that he felt he had neglected me. You see that we hadn't had a doctor before. And so doctor Gore would be the, the doctor up there that we would have.

words: So we didn't have any from Lois, and it was too hard to come out. But Raleigh felt that he had let me down, see, and that that was what was worrying him. But, we named it. words: Fat baby, but just as blue as indigo. He died, so I didn't know other Raleigh. I could talk even to some children again, but we I did. I talked for everyone that I had first. speaker: Lora Albright
words: So I talked him into the daughter. And you know, God's awfully good to me because we got Peggy Jane. Just what we ordered. Then about that time we bought this and we were building this house. words: And. And then that kid must have talked. Raleigh. I said, well, why don't we have another baby? And he said, well, he said, aren't you satisfied? And I said, no. words: So God was good to me again. And we had another little girl and another little boy. Then I call it quits. And so, Rowland, we had the two boys and the two girls, and they are married now. words: Oh, boys. Wasn't that something? No girls at all. The. All right, run the boys.
